page: Microsoft Azure D48as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dasv7 family, powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. The system is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable, allowing external storage attachments.

Benchmark data indicates strong compute and memory capabilities. The AMD EPYC processor delivers top-tier performance in stress-ng single-core CPU tests, gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression. Multi-core performance, PassMark CPU scores, and database operations rank in the top 25 percent. Memory bandwidth is strong for small cached blocks and large uncached writes, though large uncached reads fall into the average tier. For lightweight AI workloads, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds with small 135M models and strong text generation with medium 7B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D48as_v7 offers a high-density, dedicated compute profile without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The lack of local storage requires external disk provisioning, which represents a structural tradeoff for I/O-heavy workloads. This server is suited for general-purpose enterprise workloads, multi-threaded web serving, database operations, and moderate CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D48as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dasv7 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, it features 48 dedicated vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of RAM. While it lacks local storage and onboard GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable. Performance benchmarks place this instance in the top 10% for single-core CPU tasks and single-threaded compression, and in the top 25% for multi-core operations, database tasks, and Redis SET operations. Memory bandwidth is strong for small cached blocks and large uncached writes. This resource profile offers balanced cost efficiency for general-purpose enterprise workloads, multi-threaded web serving, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48as_v7 is a general-purpose VM featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs at 4.5 GHz and 192 GB RAM. It delivers top-tier single-core performance and is optimized for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48as_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core CPU performance.
tagline: A high-frequency, general-purpose virtual machine delivering dedicated AMD EPYC compute power and balanced memory for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor at 4.5 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression
Premium Storage capable with no local storage included
Best suited for database operations, web serving, and CPU-based inference
